name: Norman
notes: Norman (Nouormand), an Oïl (Gallo-Romance) language of Normandy, Jersey (Jèrriais), Guernsey (Guernésiais) and Sark. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Latin alphabet using French-based spelling conventions. It is NOT one orthography: Jèrriais, Guernésiais, Cotentinais and the continental varieties each have their own spelling systems and differing sound values for the same digraphs, and the deep etymological (French-style) spelling means a letter-to-phoneme table is highly rule- and variety-dependent. A per-variety spec (e.g. Jèrriais) would be the honest unit of work. Input is assumed to be a Latin Norman orthography, unspecified variety.
